doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_mul: multiply two MPFR values, round per rnd at the caller-supplied prec, return {value, ternary}. The C function mutates rop in place (whose prec is set independently) and returns the ternary as int; the TS port takes prec explicitly and returns the canonical Result shape from src/core.ts. Class is 'arithmetic' (50ms per-case budget): mpfr_mul is the second prototypical arithmetic op (after mpfr_add) and the last Pilot function. Multiplication is conceptually simpler than addition (one bigint product, no alignment, no cancellation, no signed-zero rounding rule) but has its own pitfalls: the product is in [2^(a.prec+b.prec-2), 2^(a.prec+b.prec)) so the result exponent depends on the bit-length of the product (carry-out bit), and the ternary direction depends on the resultSign = a.sign*b.sign — NOT just a.sign as the add port does for same-sign add. The golden's fuzz cap of prec <= 200 keeps the 200x200 -> 400-bit bigint product comfortably inside the 50ms budget.
c_signature: int mpfr_mul(mpfr_t rop, mpfr_srcptr op1, mpfr_srcptr op2, mpfr_rnd_t rnd)
divergence_from_c: C mutates rop (which carries prec) and returns the ternary as a separate int; TS takes prec as a positional argument and returns {value, ternary} per the locked Result schema (src/core.ts L173-L176). Behaviour for specials matches the C reference (mpfr/src/mul.c L52-L94 — the mpfr_mul3 dispatch on MPFR_ARE_SINGULAR): NaN * anything -> NaN; 0 * Inf or Inf * 0 -> NaN; +-Inf * +-Inf -> sign(a.sign*b.sign) * Inf, ternary 0; +-Inf * normal -> sign-product * Inf, ternary 0; +-0 * +-0 or +-0 * normal -> +-0 with sign = a.sign*b.sign, ternary 0. Normal * normal: result sign is a.sign * b.sign (NOT a.sign); product = a.mant * b.mant has bit-length in {a.prec+b.prec-1, a.prec+b.prec}; result exp = a.exp + b.exp + (bitLength - (a.prec + b.prec)), then round the (sign, exp, product) triple to prec via the same roundMantissa primitive add.ts uses.
refs: mpfr/src/mul.c L172-L237 — top-level dispatcher; the production path (mpfr_mul) is the unguarded fallthrough that delegates to mpfr_mul3 (when MPFR_WANT_ASSERT >= 2) or to mpfr_mul_1/mul_1n/mul_2/mul_3/mul_4/general (L246-L1100+, gated by GMP_NUMB_BITS == 64 and prec ranges). We mirror the contract of the general path: exact bigint product, then mpfr_round_raw with the sign-product injected.

mpfr/src/mul.c L34-L94 — mpfr_mul3: the specials dispatch and the general algorithm. Specifically L52-L94 is the MPFR_ARE_SINGULAR block — the source of every special-case behaviour the port mirrors. L96-L168 is the general algorithm we mirror: ax = exp(b) + exp(c); mpn_mul yields b1 in {0, 1} which is the MSB-of-product carry; if b1 == 0 we lshift by 1 (i.e. the product is in [2^(p+q-2), 2^(p+q-1))); round_raw produces the final mantissa and the ternary; the final exp is ax + b1 - 1 + cc (cc is the round-up carry).

mpfr/src/mul.c L269-L364 — mpfr_mul_1: the prec <= GMP_NUMB_BITS fast path. Useful as a sanity reference: confirms umul_ppmm produces the high limb a0 of the product with a0 < MPFR_LIMB_HIGHBIT iff the product's MSB is at bit position GMP_NUMB_BITS-2 rather than GMP_NUMB_BITS-1 (i.e. one bit short). This is the binary-equivalent of our bitLength check on the bigint product.
src/core.ts — locked MPFR / RoundingMode / Result / Ternary types; validate() invariants the returned value must satisfy.

src/ops/add.ts — reference for the local roundMantissa pattern, validateArgs, packNormal helper, and the same arithmetic-class structure (specials dispatch, then a normal-normal core function). The mul port duplicates these helpers locally per the 'don't extract until a third caller forces it' note in set_d.ts. add.ts is the THIRD consumer of roundMantissa (after set_d, get_d), so mul.ts is the trigger to extract — but per CLAUDE.md Rule 6 (Trivial-Small-Core) and PIL.4 (no architectural drift mid-Pilot) we keep the duplication for one more port; the extraction is filed as a follow-up.

src/internal/mpn/add_n.ts, src/internal/mpn/sub_n.ts — substrate used by add.ts. mpfr_mul has no equivalent substrate composition (the product is a single bigint multiplication; there is no mpn_mul.ts substrate in scope yet) — the bigint primitive is faithful at the I/O contract level per Law 3.

CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts' — 0 * Inf == NaN (one of the specials); ternary direction is sign of (rounded - exact); rounding-mode count is FIVE; the sign rule for mul is product-of-signs (not a.sign as in same-sign add); NaN sign is 1 by convention. mpfr_prec_t in bits not digits.
